title: Coordinates, Scalars, Vector2, Angles, and Units · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for coordinates, scalars, vector2, angles, and units and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, pygame-ce, SDL, display, input, audio, filesystem, packaging, GPU, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for unit-labeled 2D geometry model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Declare screen origin, axes, handedness, logical viewport, centers, bounds, and world-to-screen conversion.
Use Vector2 addition, subtraction, scaling, magnitude, squared distance, dot, cross, copy, and mutation with explicit meaning.
Derive facing vectors from a named zero-angle and degree convention and test clockwise screen appearance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Coordinate Convention · changed case · Quiz Coordinate Convention
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 5127 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Declare screen origin, axes, handedness, logical viewport, centers, bounds, and world-to-screen conversion.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Coordinate Convention · changed case · Quiz Coordinate Convention.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Motion Units, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Motion Units precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Motion Units, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Asteroids team handles case 1203 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Label position, velocity, acceleration, angle, angular velocity, seconds, and frame counts and reject unit-invalid formulas.

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Motion Units
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Motion Units: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 8584 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Label position, velocity, acceleration, angle, angular velocity, seconds, and frame counts and reject unit-invalid formulas.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Motion Units.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Motion Units, keep Motion Units connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Label position, velocity, acceleration, angle, angular velocity, seconds, and frame counts and reject unit-invalid formulas.
